headings.text: Titanium is a durable, strong metal with an exception strength-to-weight ratio
Titanium vs Aluminum: Workhorse Metals for Machining and 3D Printing
Lightweight Yet Durable Titanium Great Option for Aerospace Parts

images.alt: Titanium part for Helix’s folding bike frame

meta_description: Titanium is extremely strong and boasts a high strength-to-weight ratio. It also has excellent corrosion resistance, high operating temperatures (up to 1,000°F) and is nontoxic.

sections.heading: CNC Machining for Titanium
sections.text: CNC Machining for Titanium Titanium is a durable, strong metal with an exception strength-to-weight ratio Get A Quote

sections.heading: Titanium is a durable, strong metal with an exception strength-to-weight ratio
sections.text: CNC Machining for Titanium Titanium is a durable, strong metal with an exception strength-to-weight ratio Get A Quote

sections.heading: What is Titanium?
sections.text: What is Titanium? Titanium is a popular material for CNC machining due to its excellent strength-to-weight ratio, corrosion resistance, and biocompatibility. However, titanium presents unique challenges in machining due to its high strength, low thermal conductivity, and tendency to work harden during cutting. Key Benefits High strength-to-weight ratio Corrosion resistance Biocompatibility (non-toxic) End-use finishing options

sections.heading: Key Benefits
sections.text: What is Titanium? Titanium is a popular material for CNC machining due to its excellent strength-to-weight ratio, corrosion resistance, and biocompatibility. However, titanium presents unique challenges in machining due to its high strength, low thermal conductivity, and tendency to work harden during cutting. Key Benefits High strength-to-weight ratio Corrosion resistance Biocompatibility (non-toxic) End-use finishing options

sections.heading: Titanium Machining Applications
sections.text: Titanium Machining Applications Titanium has range of applications due to its advanced material properties. Frequently, you’ll find titanium parts in the aerospace, medical, military and marine industries. Compressor blades Discs Airframe and space capsule components Surgical equipment Pressure vessels

sections.heading: Finishing Options for Titanium
sections.text: Finishing Options for Titanium Anodizing is a common post-processing choice for titanium CNC parts. The anodizing process has a number of benefits: Increased corrosion resistance Minimal increase in weight Smooth surface reduces friction Enhanced appearance
